fixtures.description: Positive case: clear TypeError with stack trace — pipeline should identify NoneType comparison as root cause and produce a complete Phase 1-4 report (exercises C1 persona, C2 Iron Law requirements)

inputs.issue_description: Function 'process_user_data' raises TypeError at line 47 when handling requests with optional age field
inputs.error_text:
TypeError: '<' not supported between instances of 'NoneType' and 'int'
  File 'app/processors.py', line 47, in process_user_data
    if user.age < 18:
  File 'app/api.py', line 23, in handle_request
    result = process_user_data(request.body)
  File 'app/wsgi.py', line 8, in application
    return handle_request(environ, start_response)

fixtures.description: Positive case with recent changes: all authentication tests fail after session middleware refactor — pipeline should trace root cause to JWT migration and identify missing token validation (exercises C1, C2, C8 BACKEND/MODEL_ID)

inputs.issue_description: All 12 authentication tests fail after yesterday's session middleware refactor — returning 401 Unauthorized on valid credentials
inputs.error_text:
FAILED tests/test_auth.py::test_login_valid_credentials - AssertionError: assert 401 == 200
FAILED tests/test_auth.py::test_login_remember_me - AssertionError: assert 401 == 200
FAILED tests/test_auth.py::test_session_persistence - AssertionError: assert 401 == 200

All failures are 401 responses where 200 is expected. Tests were passing before the refactor.
inputs.recent_changes: Refactored SessionMiddleware: replaced cookie-based sessions with JWT tokens. Changed __call__ method to decode Authorization header instead of reading session cookie. Removed set_cookie() calls from login handler. Updated User.from_session() to User.from_jwt_token().

fixtures.description: Pattern analysis case with working examples (C3 reference comparison): UserService fails to send welcome email but OrderService sends confirmation emails correctly — pipeline should identify missing email_service call as root cause by comparing the two implementations

inputs.issue_description: New users are not receiving welcome emails after registration. The registration API returns 201 success but no email is sent. OrderService sends order confirmation emails correctly.
inputs.recent_changes: No recent changes to email infrastructure. Issue was discovered in production review — never worked since UserService was written 3 weeks ago.

fixtures.description: Architectural threshold case (C8 MAX_FIX_ATTEMPTS=3): distributed job queue has race condition where workers process the same job twice — 3 fix attempts each revealed new coupling problems — pipeline should detect architectural_problem pattern and recommend team discussion instead of another fix

inputs.issue_description: Race condition in distributed job queue: workers intermittently process the same job twice, causing duplicate email sends and double-charges. Occurs under load with 4+ workers.
inputs.error_text:
ERROR duplicate_job_processor: Job job_id=7823 already processed by worker-2, now processing on worker-4
ERROR payment_service: Duplicate charge detected for order_id=4421
WARNING email_service: Duplicate welcome email sent to user_id=9912
inputs.recent_changes: Fix attempt 1: Added Redis SETNX lock with 30s TTL — resolved for low load but deadlock under high concurrency when lock expires before job completes. Fix attempt 2: Changed to optimistic locking in PostgreSQL (version field) — fixed the lock issue but revealed that job_status updates across the queue/worker boundary are not atomic. Fix attempt 3: Added deduplication table with job_id unique constraint — resolved atomicity but revealed that worker heartbeat resets allow zombie workers to reclaim in-progress jobs.

fixtures.description: Intermittent/environmental case: CI pipeline randomly fails on module import roughly 3 times per week but always passes on retry — pipeline should determine reproducibility is intermittent and guide evidence gathering rather than proposing immediate fixes (exercises C1, C2 anti-premature-fix requirements)

inputs.issue_description: CI pipeline randomly fails with ModuleNotFoundError on 'pytest' approximately 3 times per week. Always passes on manual retry with no changes. Affects all branches, not correlated with any specific commit.
inputs.error_text:
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pytest'
  File '/home/runner/work/myapp/myapp/run_tests.sh', line 4, in <module>
    python -m pytest tests/

pip install exit code: 0
pip show pytest: WARNING: Package(s) not found: pytest

Note: requirements.txt contains pytest==7.4.0. Installation log shows success but package not found.

fixtures.description: Edge case: minimal input with only issue_description and no supporting context — pipeline should still run all phases and produce a report with honest uncertainty acknowledgment and evidence-gathering next steps, not premature fix proposals (exercises C1, C2 epistemic honesty requirement)

inputs.issue_description: Application crashes on startup with exit code 1 in production but not in staging

coverage_doc:
Fixture coverage (R16 — exercises C1, C2, C3, C8 = 4 distinct C-categories):

  C1 Identity (PREFIX_TEXT persona): all fixtures — persona applied via SYSTEM_PROMPT on every m.instruct call
  C2 Operating Rules (5 requirements): all fixtures — require_root_cause_before_fix, require_no_process_skipping,
    require_no_premature_fix_proposals, require_epistemic_honesty, require_fix_verification applied across phases
  C3 User Facts (reference comparison): working_example_comparison — exercises Phase 2 working_examples_text
    parameter and the C3 'compare against references' operating rule
  C8 Runtime Environment (MAX_FIX_ATTEMPTS, BACKEND, MODEL_ID): architectural_issue_detected — triggers
    fix_attempts_count >= MAX_FIX_ATTEMPTS (3) threshold and classify_failure_pattern slot;
    test_failure_after_refactor exercises BACKEND/MODEL_ID session handling
